title: Lush Indian Tomato Jalebi
description: A silky, grassy dessert that marries the sweetness of San Marzano tomatoes with the crispness of traditional jalebi, perfect for midnight cravings.
instructions: Whisk wheat flour, semolina, yogurt, egg yolk, baking powder, cardamom and saffron with enough water to make a smooth, thick batter.
Heat a deep pan over medium heat and add ghee, ensuring the pan is evenly coated before the batter.
Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot ghee, forming small, circular rings, and fry until golden and crisp.
Once fried, remove the jalebis and let them drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Blend the San Marzano tomatoes into a smooth puree, then stir in sugar until it dissolves completely.
Heat the tomato-sugar mixture in a saucepan until it reaches a gentle boil, then simmer for 5 minutes to thicken slightly.
Allow the tomato syrup to cool to room temperature before using it for drizzling over the jalebis.
Arrange the fried jalebis on a serving plate and drizzle the tomato syrup generously over each piece.
Garnish with chopped mint leaves for a fresh, grassy note.
Serve immediately, ensuring the texture remains silky and the flavors are balanced.
